cards.label: 01 · v0
cards.title: v0 ships shadcn out of the box.
cards.body: Every v0 output is Next.js + Tailwind + shadcn. The code is clean enough to copy into a real Vercel project without rework. Best for individual components and polished landing pages.

cards.label: 02 · lovable
cards.title: Lovable wires auth + DB in the same prompt.
cards.body: Tell Lovable "build me a Twitter clone with login." 10 min later: Supabase auth, Postgres schema, deployed URL, working signup. Frontend ships, backend ships, infra ships — all together.

cards.label: 03 · pick
cards.title: Pick v0 for components.
cards.body: If your existing app is already on Vercel + Next.js + shadcn — and you just need a fresh landing section, hero, or feature page — v0 outputs slot in cleanly. Lovable is overkill.

cards.label: 04 · pick
cards.title: Pick Lovable for whole apps.
cards.body: Zero-to-MVP in one session: idea → working web app with auth + DB + deploy. Trade: the output is harder to port out cleanly if you want to leave the platform later.

cards.label: 05 · oss
cards.title: Open-source alt: Bolt.new + Bolt.diy.
cards.body: Both v0 and Lovable have OSS-spirit alternatives. Bolt.new (hosted, generous free tier) and Bolt.diy (fully self-hosted, BYO API key) cover the same ground if you want zero subscription lock-in.

take.use_if: You're picking between v0 and Lovable for a NEW project today — match the tool to whether you need components or a whole app.
take.skip_if: You already have an opinionated workflow with Cursor / Claude Code — adding a generator on top usually adds friction.
